label: 12 Server Buffer
info:
NODE: Modbus-Server (buffer slave)

FEATURES IN THIS FLOW
- In-package TCP Modbus slave backed by register buffers
- Inject writes into holding / coils / input / discrete buffers
- Five outputs mirror activity (not a per-request flow gateway)

WHAT THE CONFIG DOES
- serverPort / hostname → TCP listen endpoint
- coilsBufferSize / holdingBufferSize / … → buffer lengths
- Inject payload: { value, register, address, disableMsgOutput }
- register: 'holding' | 'coils' | 'input' | 'discrete'

HOW TO RUN
1) Deploy
2) Click Fill holding → writes [11,22,33,44] into holding from address 0
3) Attach a Modbus-Client (other examples) to read the buffer back

Demo TCP port: 10514

NOT a flow-based request/response gateway — see example 15 and GitHub #567.

Config nodes are flow-scoped: delete this tab to remove client/IO config.

name: holding buf
func:
msg.payload = { value: [11, 22, 33, 44], register: 'holding', address: 0, disableMsgOutput: 0 };
return msg;
